susceptibility.title: The Role of Self-Reporting in Health

woEO.body: Self-reporting is necessary for certain health information because it cannot be automatically monitored or detected, such as dietary intake, physical activity, and symptoms. Although self-reporting can be subjective and prone to memory bias, it allows individuals to provide information on their experiences and behaviors, which can inform healthcare professionals and researchers about their health status and guide interventions.

wEO.title: What is "honest reporting"?
wEO.lead: Have you ever shared the truth about your dietary habits with people around you, such as your doctor, family, or friends? You may not realize it yet, but every honest answer is a powerful step toward your ideal health.
wEO.engagement: Next, we will share some information, so if you're feeling a bit tired right now, please stay engaged as much as possible!
scenarioSteps: One day, feeling eager to take charge of your wellbeing, you walk into the hospital to see a doctor, ready to make progress.
After the consultation, the doctor mentions that your physical condition is closely related to your recent diet and asks what you have been eating lately.
In reality, you've been filling up on cookies, sweets, and instant noodles recently due to laziness.

You pause, thinking that if you share the truth honestly, you're excited that the doctor will be positive, and think that it's natural for you to step closer to the version of yourself you've been hoping to become.
With that in mind, you say that you've been mostly indulging in snacks recently, with only occasional healthy meals.
The doctor understands your real situation and prescribes relevant medication and medical advice based on that, helping you to align your health goals.

woEO.framing: Self-reporting plays out across many small moments in everyday life — a logged meal, a noted symptom, a recalled walk. On the next page, we'll look at the tools that help people do this consistently and accurately.
woEO.heading: Self-Reporting Tools
woEO.intro: Here are some popular methods used worldwide for self-reporting:

tools.label: Paper diaries
tools.body: Patients record their daily activities, such as food intake, physical activity, and symptom severity, in a physical notebook or diary.

tools.label: Electronic diaries
tools.body: Similar to paper diaries, but patients use electronic devices, such as smartphones or tablets, to record their activities.

tools.label: Questionnaires
tools.body: Patients answer a set of standardized questions designed to capture specific health-related information, such as quality of life, mood, or pain intensity.

tools.label: Wearable devices
tools.body: Electronic devices that patients wear on their bodies, such as fitness trackers or smartwatches, that track various health-related metrics, such as heart rate, sleep quality, and steps taken.

tools.label: Mobile apps
tools.body: A variety of mobile apps allow patients to track their health-related behaviors, such as food intake and physical activity, and sync the data with their healthcare provider.

woEO.outro: In addition, it is recommended to consider the objectives and target population before choosing the type of tool, in order to maximize its effectiveness.

wEO.heading: Rewards of Honest Reporting (Using Honest Reporting of Diet as an Example)

section1.title: Strong Mental Health and Healthy Eating Habits
section1.body: Honest reporting of your diet can bring positive feelings and even strengthen your mental health, further nurturing feelings of confidence and peace about your body image and eating habits. This may contribute to the development or improvement of your food choices — eating enough fruit, consuming whole-grain foods, eating vegetables, etc. — providing a stronger sense of mastery over food. These habits can create a virtuous cycle where honest reporting compounds.

section2.title: Receiving Tailored Guidance and Unlocking Health Gains
section2.body: When you honestly report your diet, let's consider the example of a doctor: they can precisely tailor your medication and recommendations thanks to the fully accurate information. Better still, your body may fully benefit from these adjustments, unlocking several gains — including smooth food–medication compatibility, balanced nutrition, stable blood-sugar levels, lower risk of hypertension, and steady improvement of your overall condition.

efficacy.title: How to Unlock the Benefits of Honest Reporting of Your Eating Habits

responseEfficacy.heading: Be Honest with Yourself First
responseEfficacy.body: Each time you share your truth, it's a gift to your own well-being and opens up new opportunities for growth. Only when you embrace the truth can you truly unlock the full potential of your health. Let honesty bring you closer to achieving a healthy and fulfilling life.

items.label: Pause and Reflect
items.body: Stop for a moment and take a deep breath.

items.label: Recall Your Why
items.body: Remind yourself of why you want to embrace honest reporting. Speak affirmations like "I am willing to do what it takes to live the life I want."

items.label: Reach Out to Your Support System
items.body: Find people who can offer helpful suggestions to help you get back on track.

doctorPreference.title: Doctor Preferences
doctorPreference.lead: What type of doctors are you looking for regarding {disease}? Please provide your preferences, and we will recommend suitable doctors from our network.

questions.text: Do you have a preference for the communication style of the doctors?
Do you prefer a particular pace for the medical consultations?
What specific information about {disease} would you like to know more about?

doctorPreference.validationHint: Please answer all 3 questions before continuing.

doctorRecommendation.title: Based on your preferences, we recommend Dr. Zhang for your treatment
doctorRecommendation.body: During Dr. Zhang's leisured consultation, she will provide medication for treating {disease}, guidance on preventive measures in daily life, and information about causes of the condition.
doctorRecommendation.note: Please note: Dr. Zhang emphasizes a fundamental treatment approach, and she may have strict expectations regarding your lifestyle habits. It is preferred that you have already made some improvements in your diet and lifestyle habits before seeking treatment, to avoid unnecessary utilization of healthcare resources.
reminders: Dr. Zhang will ask you to report your lifestyle and dietary behaviors during the consultation to facilitate assessment.
Adhere to Dr. Zhang's recommendations during your next visit. If non-compliant, you will be asked to make improvements before rescheduling the appointment.

consultationReminder.title: Initial Consultation Reminder: Report your dietary behaviors over the past week.
consultationReminder.intro: On the next page, please provide your dietary behaviors in the past week to facilitate Dr. Zhang's assessment of your {disease} issue.
consultationReminder.explain: Afterward, Dr. Zhang will evaluate whether your dietary behaviors can improve or worsen your {disease} based on your answers.
sliderInstructions: On the next page, please adjust the slider according to your level of agreement with the statements.
You must use the slider to answer the questions.
If your answer matches the slider's initial position, please tap on that position so the system can recognize that you have responded.
consultationReminder.imageAlt: Doctor with stethoscope holding a tablet across from a patient

practice.title: Slider Practice

intro.lead: Each question's slider starts at a fixed default position. Drag the slider to the answer you want — and {strong}, you must still tap that spot once so the system can register your response. Then click the ✓ button on the bottom-right to confirm. Once confirmed, your answer cannot be modified.
intro.leadStrong: even if the default position already matches your answer
intro.ramNoticeWoRAM: On this study, every slider starts at the right side. Try answering the practice question below — including a tap on the slider even when the default already matches your answer.
intro.ramNoticeWRAM: On this study, every slider starts at the left side. Try answering the practice question below — including a tap on the slider even when the default already matches your answer.

practice.question: Do you understand how the slider works? *

survey.title: Dietary Behavior Survey
survey.uidWarning: ⚠ User ID not found. Please enter this page via the provided link.

intro.h2: Report your dietary behavior over the past week
intro.p: Please use the slider to indicate your level of agreement with each statement (1 = lowest, 7 = highest).

survey.validationHint: Please confirm all items before continuing ({done}/{total} confirmed)

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat three meals regularly?
questions.minLabel: I never ate three meals regularly in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I ate three meals regularly every day in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat candy or snacks?
questions.minLabel: I ate candy or snacks every day in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I never ate candy or snacks in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you chew your food thoroughly, chewing at least 20 times per bite before swallowing?
questions.minLabel: I never chewed my food thoroughly before swallowing in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I chewed at least 20 times per bite in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, when you were thirsty or it was hot, did you drink unhealthy beverages (sugary or alcoholic) besides plain water?
questions.minLabel: I drank unhealthy beverages every day in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I drank only plain water in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat fried or greasy foods (e.g., peanuts, chips, fried chicken)?
questions.minLabel: I ate fried foods every day in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I never ate fried foods in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat fruit every day?
questions.minLabel: I never ate fruit in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I ate fruit every day in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat green vegetables every day?
questions.minLabel: I never ate green vegetables in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I ate green vegetables every day in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat late-night snacks every day?
questions.minLabel: I ate late-night snacks every day in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I never ate late-night snacks in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you eat while watching TV or using a tablet, phone, or computer?
questions.minLabel: I was distracted while eating in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I focused on eating in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, when you were in a bad mood, did you eat to make yourself feel better?
questions.minLabel: I ate to improve my mood every time in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I never ate to improve my mood in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you use food as a reward or as a way to celebrate?
questions.minLabel: I used food as a reward every time in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I never used food as a reward in the past seven days

questions.text: In the past week, did you wait until you were very hungry to go grocery shopping?
questions.minLabel: I always waited until I was very hungry to shop in the past seven days
questions.maxLabel: I never waited until I was very hungry to shop in the past seven days
